events.label: reasoning
events.text: The scenario describes slings over unprotected edges in a rigging configuration. Both ASME B30 and OSHA 1926.251 require protection of slings from sharp edges/corners. Slings over unprotected edges is a clear non-compliance.

events.label: response
events.text:

  "decision": "fail",

  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],

  "findings": [
    "Slings are rigged over unprotected/sharp edges without edge protection, risking cutting, abrasion, or failure of the sling",
    "Rigging configuration does not meet ASME B30 and OSHA 1926.251 requirements for protecting slings from damage at contact points"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop the lift and remove load from affected slings",
    "Install suitable edge protection (riggers pads, corner saddles, or softeners) at all sling-to-edge contact points",
    "Re-inspect slings for damage before returning to service",
    "Have competent rigger reconfigure the lift and verify compliance before resuming work"
  ],
  "rationale": "Both ASME B30 and OSHA 1926.251 require that slings be protected from cutting and abrasion at sharp corners or unprotected edges. Rigging slings over unprotected edges is a direct non-compliance and a high-risk condition that can lead to sling failure and dropped-load incidents.",
  "references": [
    "ASME B30.9 (Slings) - protection from sharp edges/corners",
    "ASME B30.26 (Rigging Hardware) - rigging practices",
    "OSHA 1926.251 - Rigging equipment for material handling, sling protection requirements"
  ]
}
